sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports increased fatigue, particularly at the end of the day, and difficulty keeping up with his usual bike rides, now limited to 20-30 miles. He also notes pursed lip breathing during exhalation and a palpable nodule in his right testicle. He describes feeling frustrated by his friend's faster pace on the bike and mentions a desire to perform routine testicular exams.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals low exhalation phase, pursed lip breathing, and a 2-3 mm palpable nodule in the right testicle. Vital signs are within normal limits. Neurological exam is unremarkable.
medical_concepts: palpable nodule in the right testicle

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's fatigue and pursed lip breathing raise concern for potential respiratory or cardiovascular issues, warranting further investigation. The testicular nodule requires evaluation by urology.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Order a pulmonary function test, echocardiogram, and stress test. Refer to urology for evaluation of the testicular nodule. Schedule a follow-up with cardiology, specifically Dr. Vincent Sanchez. Recommend obtaining the MMR vaccine and shingles vaccine.

medical_concepts: palpable nodule in the right testicle
